weekly_top5.title: Adversarial Flow Matching for End-to-End Autonomous Driving

weekly_top5.title: Cut-In Gap Acceptance: Comparing Autonomous Vehicles and Human Drivers in Forced Merging Scenarios

weekly_top5.title: World Model for Robot Learning: A Comprehensive Survey
weekly_top5.reason: 机器人学习世界模型全面综述，涵盖导航和自动驾驶应用

weekly_top5.title: Being-H0.7: A Latent World-Action Model from Egocentric Videos

papers.title: Unified Map Prior Encoder for Mapping and Planning
papers.abstract: We present UMPE, a Unified Map Prior Encoder that can ingest any subset of four priors (HD/SD vector maps, rasterized SD maps, satellite imagery) and fuse them with BEV features for both mapping and planning. UMPE has two branches: a vector encoder with SE(2) polyline correction and confidence-aware cross-attention, and a raster encoder with FiLM conditioning and micro-alignment. On nuScenes, UMPE achieves 63.6 mIoU on the online map segmentation task and 0.43m avg. planning error on the NMP motion planning benchmark, setting a new SOTA for map-prior-based E2E driving.

papers.title: Adversarial Flow Matching for End-to-End Autonomous Driving
papers.abstract: Autonomous driving is evolving towards end-to-end frameworks through monolithic models like VLA and specialized modular architectures. Despite divergent designs, both rely on Transformer backbones for complex reasoning, causing a shared vulnerability: visually imperceptible perturbations can manipulate E2E AD models into hazardous maneuvers by targeting the Transformer module. Most existing adversarial attacks target modular perception, but E2E attack approaches remain underexplored. We propose Adversarial Flow Matching (AFM) to generate cross-model adversarial perturbations that exploit this shared vulnerability, demonstrating through extensive experiments that both VLA and modular E2E AD models are susceptible to the same threat patterns.

papers.title: Cut-In Gap Acceptance: Comparing Autonomous Vehicles and Human Drivers in Forced Merging Scenarios
papers.abstract: We investigate how autonomous vehicles and human drivers differ in their cut-in gap acceptance behavior during forced merging scenarios. Using a combination of naturalistic driving data and controlled simulation experiments, we find that AVs exhibit more conservative gap acceptance thresholds, leading to longer merge times and increased traffic flow disruption. We propose a framework that bridges this behavioral gap while maintaining safety constraints.

papers.title: LiteVLA-H: Lightweight Vision-Language-Action Model for Aerial Guidance
papers.abstract: We present LiteVLA-H, a lightweight VLA model for aerial vehicle guidance that processes visual inputs with language commands to generate navigation actions. The model achieves real-time performance on embedded platforms while maintaining competitive accuracy.

papers.title: HERMES++: Toward a Unified Driving World Model for 3D Scene Understanding and Generation
papers.abstract: Driving world models serve as a pivotal technology for autonomous driving by simulating environmental dynamics. However, existing approaches predominantly focus on future scene generation, often overlooking comprehensive 3D scene understanding.

papers.title: GSDrive: Reinforcing Driving Policies by Multi-mode Trajectory Probing with 3D Gaussian Splatting Environment
papers.abstract: End-to-end (E2E) autonomous driving presents a promising approach for translating perceptual inputs directly into driving actions. However, prohibitive annotation costs and temporal data quality degradation hinder long-term real-world deployment.

papers.title: ProDrive: Proactive Planning for Autonomous Driving via Ego-Environment Co-Evolution
papers.abstract: End-to-end autonomous driving planners typically generate trajectories from current observations alone. However, real-world driving is highly dynamic, and such reactive planning cannot anticipate future scene evolution, often leading to myopic decisions.

papers.title: UniAda: Universal Adaptive Multi-objective Adversarial Attack for End-to-End Autonomous Driving Systems
papers.abstract: Adversarial attacks play a pivotal role in testing and improving the reliability of deep learning (DL) systems. Existing literature has demonstrated that subtle perturbations to the input can elicit erroneous outcomes in E2E driving systems.

papers.title: Reasoning About Traversability: Language-Guided Off-Road 3D Trajectory Planning
papers.abstract: While Vision-Language Models (VLMs) enable high-level semantic reasoning for end-to-end autonomous driving, particularly in unstructured environments, existing off-road datasets suffer from language annotations that are weakly aligned with vehicle actions.

papers.title: SpanVLA: Efficient Action Bridging and Learning from Negative-Recovery Samples for Vision-Language-Action Model
papers.abstract: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models offer a promising autonomous driving paradigm for leveraging world knowledge and reasoning capabilities, especially in long-tail scenarios. However, existing VLA models often struggle with high latency in action generation.

papers.title: XEmbodied: A Foundation Model with Enhanced Geometric and Physical Cues for Large-Scale Embodied Environments
papers.abstract: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models drive next-generation autonomous systems, but training them requires scalable, high-quality annotations from complex environments.

papers.title: AEGIS: Anchor-Enforced Gradient Isolation for Knowledge-Preserving Vision-Language-Action Fine-Tuning
papers.abstract: Adapting pre-trained vision-language models (VLMs) for robotic control requires injecting high-magnitude continuous gradients from a flow-matching action expert into a backbone trained exclusively with cross-entropy.

papers.title: FeaXDrive: Feasibility-aware Trajectory-Centric Diffusion Planning for End-to-End Autonomous Driving
papers.abstract: End-to-end diffusion planning has shown strong potential for autonomous driving, but the physical feasibility of generated trajectories remains insufficiently addressed.

papers.title: Unveiling the Surprising Efficacy of Navigation Understanding in End-to-End Autonomous Driving
papers.abstract: Global navigation information and local scene understanding are two crucial components of autonomous driving systems. However, many E2E driving systems tend to over-rely on local scene understanding while neglecting navigation information.

papers.title: OccDirector: Language-Guided Behavior and Interaction Generation in 4D Occupancy Space
papers.abstract: Generative world models increasingly rely on 4D occupancy for realistic autonomous driving simulation. However, existing generation frameworks depend on rigid geometric conditions or simplistic attribute-level text.

papers.title: X-Cache: Cross-Chunk Block Caching for Few-Step Autoregressive World Models Inference
papers.abstract: Real-time world simulation is becoming a key infrastructure for scalable evaluation and online RL of autonomous driving systems. Recent driving world models built on autoregressive video diffusion achieve high-fidelity, controllable generation.

papers.title: OneVL: One-Step Latent Reasoning and Planning with Vision-Language Explanation
papers.abstract: Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning has become a powerful driver of trajectory prediction in VLA-based autonomous driving, yet its autoregressive nature imposes a latency cost prohibitive for real-time deployment.

papers.title: OneDrive: Unified Multi-Paradigm Driving with Vision-Language-Action Models
papers.abstract: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) excel at autoregressive text generation, yet end-to-end autonomous driving requires multi-task learning with structured outputs and heterogeneous decoding behaviors.

papers.title: Infrastructure-Centric World Models: Bridging Temporal Depth and Spatial Breadth for Roadside Perception
papers.abstract: World models, generative AI systems that simulate how environments evolve, are transforming autonomous driving, yet all existing approaches adopt an ego-vehicle perspective, leaving the infrastructure viewpoint unexplored.

papers.title: WM-DAgger: Enabling Efficient Data Aggregation for Imitation Learning with World Models
papers.abstract: Imitation learning is a powerful paradigm for training robotic policies, yet its performance is limited by compounding errors: minor policy inaccuracies could drive robots into unseen out-of-distribution (OOD) states.

papers.title: Multi-ORFT: Stable Online Reinforcement Fine-Tuning for Multi-Agent Diffusion Planning in Cooperative Driving
papers.abstract: Closed-loop cooperative driving requires planners that generate realistic multimodal multi-agent trajectories while improving safety and traffic efficiency.

papers.title: Judge, Then Drive: A Critic-Centric Vision Language Action Framework for Autonomous Driving
papers.abstract: Recent advances in vision language action (VLA) models have shown remarkable potential for autonomous driving by directly mapping multimodal inputs to control signals. However, previous VLA-based methods have not explicitly exploited the critic capability of VLAs to refine driving decisions.

papers.title: Do World Action Models Generalize Better than VLAs? A Robustness Study
papers.abstract: Robot action planning in the real world requires not only understanding the current state but also predicting how it will evolve. Vision-language-action (VLA) models have achieved notable success, but we compare their generalization against world-action models.

papers.title: Frozen LLMs as Map-Aware Spatio-Temporal Reasoners for Vehicle Trajectory Prediction
papers.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have recently demonstrated strong reasoning capabilities in the field of autonomous driving. This study introduces a framework to evaluate frozen LLMs as spatio-temporal reasoners for vehicle trajectory prediction.

papers.title: Dreaming Across Towns: Semantic Rollout and Town-Adversarial Regularization for Zero-Shot Held-Out-Town Fixed-Route Driving in CARLA
papers.abstract: Learned driving agents often degrade when deployed in unseen environments. This paper studies zero-shot transfer of a closed-loop fixed-route driving agent across CARLA towns.

papers.title: LaST-R1: Reinforcing Action via Adaptive Physical Latent Reasoning for VLA Models
papers.abstract: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have increasingly incorporated reasoning mechanisms for complex robotic manipulation. We propose LaST-R1 with adaptive physical latent reasoning.

papers.title: Understanding Adversarial Transferability in Vision-Language Models for Autonomous Driving
papers.abstract: Vision-language models (VLMs) are increasingly used in autonomous driving. We study whether adversarial attacks transfer across different VLM architectures.

papers.title: PALCAS: A Priority-Aware Intelligent Lane Change Advisory System for Autonomous Vehicles
papers.abstract: We present a priority-aware intelligent lane change advisory system based on multi-agent federated reinforcement learning for autonomous vehicles.

papers.title: FreeOcc: Training-Free Embodied Open-Vocabulary Occupancy Prediction
papers.abstract: We present FreeOcc, a training-free framework for open-vocabulary occupancy prediction from monocular or RGB-D sequences.

papers.title: Do Open-Loop Metrics Predict Closed-Loop Driving? A Cross-Benchmark Correlation Study of NAVSIM and Bench2Drive
papers.relevance: NAVSIM与Bench2Drive跨benchmark相关性研究，验证开环指标对闭环驾驶性能的预测能力

papers.title: Being-H0.7: A Latent World-Action Model from Egocentric Videos
papers.relevance: Latent world-action model从第一视角视频学习动力学和任务进度表征，解决VLA shortcut mapping问题

papers.title: World Model for Robot Learning: A Comprehensive Survey
papers.relevance: 世界模型在机器人学习中的全面综述，覆盖策略学习、规划、仿真、评估、数据生成

papers.title: An End-to-End Decision-Aware Multi-Scale Attention-Based Model for Explainable Autonomous Driving
papers.relevance: 决策感知多尺度注意力可解释自动驾驶模型

papers.title: Physically Native World Models: A Hamiltonian Perspective on Generative World Modeling
papers.relevance: 哈密顿力学视角的世界模型，对自动驾驶物理一致性世界模型有重要启发

papers.title: Linking Behaviour and Perception to Evaluate Meaningful Human Control over Partially Automated Driving
papers.relevance: 部分自动驾驶中人类有意义控制(MHC)的行为感知评估

papers.title: Learning physically grounded traffic accident reconstruction from public accident reports
papers.relevance: 从公开事故报告学习物理层面的交通事故重建

papers.title: Embodied Interpretability: Linking Causal Understanding to Generalization in Vision-Language-Action Models
papers.relevance: 提出Interventional Significance Score (ISS)和Nuisance Mass Ratio (NMR)，分析VLA策略的因果归因和泛化能力

papers.title: Learning from the Unseen: Generative Data Augmentation for Geometric-Semantic Accident Anticipation
papers.relevance: 双路径框架：视频合成管线生成高保真驾驶场景 + GNN增强语义推理
